lede: A look at December 31 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Ryan Flamingo and Jonathan Horton, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: COVID-19 vaccine
events.desc: The World Health Organization issues its first emergency use validation for a COVID-19 vaccine.

events.title: Taipei 101
events.desc: The official opening of Taipei 101, the tallest skyscraper at that time in the world, standing at a height of 509 metres (1,670 ft).

events.title: European Exchange Rate Mechanism
events.desc: The European Exchange Rate Mechanism freezes the values of the legacy currencies in the Eurozone, and establishes the value of the euro currency.

events.title: Dupont Plaza Hotel arson
events.desc: Three disgruntled employees set fire to the Dupont Plaza Hotel in San Juan, Puerto Rico, killing more than 90 people and injuring 140 others, making it the second-deadliest hotel fire in American history.

events.title: Jean-Bédel Bokassa
events.desc: Jean-Bédel Bokassa, leader of the Central African Republic army, and his military officers begin a coup d'état against the government of President David Dacko.

events.title: RTÉ
events.desc: RTÉ, Ireland's state broadcaster, launches its first national television service.

events.title: Times Square Ball
events.desc: The first ever ball drop in Times Square.

events.title: Queen Victoria
events.desc: Queen Victoria chooses Ottawa, then a small logging town, as the capital of the Province of Canada.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: Battle of Quebec: British forces under General Guy Carleton repulse an attack by Continental Army General Richard Montgomery in a snowstorm.

events.title: Arthur Guinness
events.desc: Arthur Guinness signs a 9,000-year lease at £45 per annum and starts brewing Guinness.

deaths.text: Arnold Rüütel, Estonian politician, 3rd President of Estonia
Roberto Clemente, Puerto Rican-American baseball player and Marine
Henry Gerber, German-American activist, founded the Society for Human Rights

observances.text: Christian feast day: Pope Sylvester I (Catholic Church)
New Year's Eve (International observance), and its related observances: First Night (United States)
New Year's Eve (International observance), and its related observances: Last Day of the Year or Bisperás ng Bagong Taón, special holiday between Rizal Day and New Year's Day (Philippines)
New Year's Eve (International observance), and its related observances: Novy God Eve (Russia)
New Year's Eve (International observance), and its related observances: Ōmisoka (Japan)
New Year's Eve (International observance), and its related observances: The first day of Hogmanay or "Auld Year's Night" (Scotland)
